text: Fire accidents pose a significant threat to the infrastructure of the smart cities. Early fire detection helps in saving the valuable lives and preventing the damage to properties. The existing sensor based fire detection systems are ineffective due to the delayed response, limited coverage area, frequent false alarms etc. In this paper, an efficient real time fire detection system namely Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS) is proposed using the state of the art YOLOv8 algorithm for detection of fire. The proposed system is embedded into a multi layered smart city architecture consisting of IoT layer, Fog layer, Cloud layer and Application layer in order to achieve real time processing. The existing systems generally consider few classes of fire and smoke. However, in this paper, the proposed system is evaluated for a large dataset of 26520 images comprising of various classes of fire and smoke scenarios for different ambient conditions such as light and noise. The SFDS achieves an average precision of 97.1 % and an average recall of 99.3 % for the class of smoke. The proposed YOLOv8 based system is efficient in terms of reduction of false alarms. The proposed system provides a cost effective solution for public safety, forest monitoring and intelligent security systems.
